title: acinos alpinus subsp. pyrenaeus
intro:
Acinos alpinus subsp. pyrenaeus, also known as Pyrenean thyme or Alpine acinos, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, characterized by its small, pinkish-white flowers and fragrant foliage.

This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Acinos alpinus subsp. pyrenaeus include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce stress and anxiety, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a popular choice in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Acinos alpinus subsp. pyrenaeus include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to promote relaxation, improve respiratory health, and boost the immune system.
